tool_args.label: 2020 Support to Mass Transit
tool_args.raw_text: In 2020, MTA Bridges and Tunnels (B&T) generated $830.2 million in Support to Mass Transit.

payload.description: Total Support to Mass Transit generated by B&T in 2020

evidence_refs.source_quote: In 2020, MTA Bridges and Tunnels (B&T) generated $830.2 million in Support to Mass Transit, which was $281.3 million above the Final Estimate of $548.9 million.

tool_args.label: 2020 Net Surplus
tool_args.raw_text: The net surplus of $1,219.3 million was $270.4 million higher than the Final Estimate.

payload.description: 2020 net surplus (excluding subsidies and debt service)

evidence_refs.source_quote: The net surplus of $1,219.3 million was $270.4 million higher than the Final Estimate due to $48.3 million in lower operating expenses and $222.0 million in higher revenue.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Toll revenue reached $1,639.8 million, which was $220.5 million, or 15.5% above forecast due to better than forecasted traffic projections based on the McKinsey analysis.

tool_args.label: 2020 Overtime Spending
tool_args.raw_text: Actual Overtime spending in 2020 was at a notable low. Overtime needs were estimated at $26.565 million in the Adopted Budget and actual costs came in at $14.55 million, which was 45.252% lower.

payload.description: 2020 actual overtime spending

evidence_refs.source_quote: Actual Overtime spending in 2020 was at a notable low. Overtime needs were estimated at $26.565 million in the Adopted Budget and actual costs came in at $14.55 million, which was 45.252% lower due to scheduling, deployment, and managerial efficiencies.

tool_args.label: 2020 Collision with Injuries Rate
tool_args.raw_text: Adjusting for annual traffic, the rate of collisions with injuries per million vehicles was 0.70 in 2020, an improvement over the previous year.

payload.unit: collisions with injuries per million vehicles
payload.description: Rate of collisions with injuries per million vehicles in 2020

unit_normalized.raw_text: collisions with injuries per million vehicles

evidence_refs.source_quote: Adjusting for annual traffic, the rate of collisions with injuries per million vehicles was 0.70 in 2020, an improvement over the previous year.

tool_args.label: Harlem River Drive Direct Connector Ramp at RFK Bridge Opening
tool_args.raw_text: In late 2020 B&T opened the newly constructed Harlem River Drive Direct Connector Ramp at the Robert F. Kennedy Bridge, which resulted in a significant improvement to traffic flow in the area via a direct connection from the JFK to the Northbound Harlem River Drive.

payload.description: Opening of the Harlem River Drive Direct Connector Ramp at the Robert F. Kennedy Bridge, providing direct connection from the JFK to the Northbound Harlem River Drive

evidence_refs.source_quote: In late 2020 B&T opened the newly constructed Harlem River Drive Direct Connector Ramp at the Robert F. Kennedy Bridge, which resulted in a significant improvement to traffic flow in the area via a direct connection from the JFK to the Northbound Harlem River Drive.

tool_args.label: Gowanus Expressway Widening at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: Also in late 2020, the section of the Gowanus adjoining the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB) was widened with a fourth lane eliminating a bottleneck and improving Eastbound traffic on the lower level of the VNB.

payload.description: Widening of the Gowanus Expressway section adjoining the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge with a fourth lane, eliminating a bottleneck

evidence_refs.source_quote: Also in late 2020, the section of the Gowanus adjoining the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB) was widened with a fourth lane eliminating a bottleneck and improving Eastbound traffic on the lower level of the VNB.

tool_args.label: Split Tolling Implemented at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: Split Tolling was implemented at the VNB on December 1, 2020. This configuration now tolls both directions of traffic for the first time since 1986.

payload.description: Split Tolling implemented at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge, tolling both directions of traffic for the first time since 1986

evidence_refs.source_quote: Split Tolling was implemented at the VNB on December 1, 2020. This configuration now tolls both directions of traffic for the first time since 1986.

tool_args.label: Cashless Tolling Benefits
tool_args.raw_text: The uninterrupted flow of traffic resulting from cashless tolling continued to yield sustained improvements in travel times for drivers across the agency's tolling areas. The reduced congestion in a cashless tolling environment has benefited stakeholders throughout the travel region, leading to overall improvements in safety at crossings and a reduction in motorists' idling time, thereby saving energy and reducing carbon emissions.

payload.claim_text: The uninterrupted flow of traffic resulting from cashless tolling continued to yield sustained improvements in travel times for drivers across the agency's tolling areas. The reduced congestion in a cashless tolling environment has benefited stakeholders throughout the travel region, leading to overall improvements in safety at crossings and a reduction in motorists' idling time, thereby saving energy and reducing carbon emissions.
payload.description: Cashless tolling benefits stated by B&T in 2020 year-end review

evidence_refs.source_quote: The uninterrupted flow of traffic resulting from cashless tolling continued to yield sustained improvements in travel times for drivers across the agency's tolling areas.

tool_args.label: 2020 Additional Savings Actions
tool_args.raw_text: Additional Savings Actions were taken to partially address MTA financial shortfalls, with savings starting in the second half of 2020. The actions consisted of reductions to controllable overtime, consulting services, and non-essential, non-service expenses. These reductions resulted in savings of $19.2 million from the Adopted Budget to the Final Estimate.

payload.claim_text: Additional Savings Actions were taken to partially address MTA financial shortfalls, with savings starting in the second half of 2020. The actions consisted of reductions to controllable overtime, consulting services, and non-essential, non-service expenses. These reductions resulted in savings of $19.2 million from the Adopted Budget to the Final Estimate.
payload.description: Additional Savings Actions taken in 2020 to address MTA financial shortfalls

evidence_refs.source_quote: Additional Savings Actions were taken to partially address MTA financial shortfalls, with savings starting in the second half of 2020. The actions consisted of reductions to controllable overtime, consulting services, and non-essential, non-service expenses. These reductions resulted in savings of $19.2 million from the Adopted Budget to the Final Estimate.
